description: The July 25 main event is one of the highlights of the sport's summer, and features a clash of the generations as Calandagan, Masquerade Ball, and Benvenuto Cellini square off.

description: Scottish Lassie has won her past two Saratoga Race Course starts by a combined 24 lengths, and she aims to add to that margin July 24 in the $250,000 Shuvee Stakes (G2).

description: A rundown of this week’s horse racing coverage on TV, streaming, and radio as compiled by America's Best Racing.

description: Breeders' Cup Charities' Annual Day of Giving returns July 22, exactly 100 days before the Breeders' Cup World Championships at Keeneland. A $100,000 matching gift from Ken Freirich will double donations in support of Thoroughbred industry charities.

description: Effusive and Salahudin are BloodHorse Maiden Watch Hot Prospects after victorious debuts last week.

description: The July 20 Hawthorne Race Course bankruptcy hearing provided a triple whammy for horsemen: Racing is over; there is no money to pay some $1 million in purses due to them; and they have to clear out from their backstretch home by Aug. 31.

title_en: Santa Anita Releases Autumn Meet Stakes Schedule
description: The 2026 Autumn Meet at Santa Anita Park will feature 26 stakes races that includes seven "Win and You're In" events for this year's Breeders' Cup. Santa Anita's 19-day Autumn Meet opens Sept. 25 and runs through Nov. 1.

description: On the July 20 episode of BloodHorse Monday: Lindsay Schultz on winning the Haskell Stakes (G1) with Baby Vino, Umberto Rispoli takes us through Journalism's San Diego Handicap (G2), and Atlantic Six's Jay Briscione on the honor of Book'em Danno Day.

title_en: Deceptive, Dam of Mizdirection, Dies at 27
description: Mizdirection won the Breeders' Cup Turf Sprint (G1) in 2012 and 2013, and retired with more than $1.7 million in earnings. She would sell later as a broodmare prospect for $2.7 million. She was the second stakes winner produced by Deceptive.

title_en: National Museum Debuts Hands-On 'Equine Eyes' Exhibit
description: Equine Eyes is a hands-on, research-driven interactive and playful experience designed to simulate how horses perceive the world.

title_en: TRF Holds 'Cocktails for Aftercare' Aug. 5 in Saratoga
description: The event is an opportunity to support the nation's oldest and largest Thoroughbred aftercare organization.

title_en: Woodbine's E.P. Taylor Turf Course to Reopen Aug. 8
description: Woodbine Entertainment announced the E.P. Taylor Turf Course is scheduled to return to racing use Aug. 8—several weeks ahead of the original Sept. 1 reopening target.

title_en: Study: Omeprazole Linked to Modest Increase in EIPH
description: The Hong Kong study involving 2,924 horses found a modest connection between daily treatments of the gastric treatment and preventative omeprazole and increased risk of exercise-induced pulmonary hemorrhaging, known commonly as bleeding.

title_en: NTRA Calls for Restoring Full Deduction on Winnings
description: Citing handle declines this year, the NTRA sent a letter July 17 to U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent calling on the Treasury and the IRS to back legislation that would restore the gambling loss provision to the previous level.

description: Next year's King George VI and Queen Elizabeth Stakes (G1) is set for another major prize money boost and will be run for a British record £2.5 million (approx. US$3,361,575), Ascot officials revealed.

title_en: Mattson, McArdle Among Canterbury Hall of Fame Class
description: The Canterbury Park Hall of Fame Class of 2026 includes Thoroughbred owner and breeder Pete Mattson and former chief veterinarian and Minnesota Racing Commission chair Dr. Camille McArdle.

title_en: G T Five Hundred Pulls 31-1 Upset in Connaught Cup
